projects.title: shaw-bkWatch
projects.valueStatement: Teams can add specialized bankruptcy automation without leaving the loan management platform they already use.
projects.objective: Resolve the switching objection by showing that BankruptcyWatch automation arrives natively inside Spectrum.
projects.audience: Existing Spectrum users and the servicing, collections, compliance, and recovery leaders who evaluate bankruptcy workflow risk.
projects.phase: Demo production — building the selected demo
projects.nextMilestone: Third i completes the selected demo for bkWatch review. Full-film production does not begin until the demo is approved.
projects.theme: Show real Spectrum first, reveal the bankruptcy-specialized mechanism working beneath it, deliver three visible handled outcomes, then return to the same specialist and real Spectrum workflow.
projects.vision: Resolve the switching objection by showing that BankruptcyWatch automation arrives natively inside Spectrum.
projects.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows. Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.
projects.storyboardNote: Final Demo Script Plan — original 45 words preserved, Jul 16, 2026

deliverables.title: Brainstorm ideas
deliverables.detail: Demo directions were explored with their own storyboards and scripts; Final Demo was selected for demo production.

deliverables.title: Demo video(s)
deliverables.detail: The selected Final Demo is currently being built. It is not complete and has not entered client review.

deliverables.title: Storyboard + script
deliverables.detail: Six-scene storyboard and finalized voiceover script ready.

deliverables.title: Prompts
deliverables.detail: 122-item instruction inventory and 42 Final Demo instructions passed quality review.

deliverables.title: Voiceover
deliverables.detail: Finalized voiceover script ready for recording.

deliverables.title: Stills
deliverables.detail: Selected-demo scene previews are in production. Supporting brainstorm directions have no media placeholders.

deliverables.title: Videos
deliverables.detail: Selected-demo motion production follows approved demo stills and product-accuracy checks.

deliverables.title: Final versions
deliverables.detail: Full-film production and final versions begin only after the demo is approved and the embedded record advances.

timeline.title: Phase 1 — Brainstorming
timeline.detail: shaw-bkWatch integration brief separated from future Film 2

timeline.title: Phase 2 — Direction selection
timeline.detail: Final Demo selected from the brainstorm directions; original 45-word script retained

timeline.title: Review 1 — Review Ideas and Choose Direction
timeline.detail: bkWatch meeting complete; Final Demo selected for demo production

timeline.title: Phase 3 — Story + Script
timeline.detail: Six scenes, 12 segments, and 122-item instruction inventory ready

timeline.title: Phase 4 — Prompts
timeline.detail: 42 Final Demo instructions passed quality review; video not started

timeline.title: Phase 5 — Build selected demo
timeline.detail: Demo stills and motion are being built scene by scene; product details and exact composite frames remain approval checkpoints

timeline.title: Review 2 — Review Demo and Finalize Direction
timeline.detail: bkWatch reviews the completed demo and approves, revises, or rejects the direction

timeline.title: Phase 6 — Full-film production
timeline.detail: After demo approval, the same embedded storyboard, media history, script, and comments record advances into full-film production

timeline.title: Review 3 — Review Draft and Give Feedback
timeline.detail: bkWatch reviews the draft full film and provides feedback before final delivery

blockers.title: Demo product-accuracy confirmations
blockers.description: Confirm the live and showable Status, Tasks, and Credit behavior while Third i builds the selected 22-second demo. This is a demo-production checkpoint, not full-film production approval.

blockers.title: Final CTA and logo hierarchy
blockers.description: Confirm the closing call-to-action and the final Shaw + BankruptcyWatch logo lockup and hierarchy used in the end scene.

scope.note: The selected demo is currently being built inside the project page to prove the visual system and direction. After bkWatch approves it, this same embedded storyboard becomes the canonical full-film production record. A complete film adds refinement passes, product-accuracy review, animation continuity, sound, and final quality review. Third i confirms full-production scope before that transition.
scope.source: Third i pricing approach — outcome/scope-led; currency shown only when confirmed

film.coreMessage: Teams can add specialized bankruptcy automation without leaving the loan management platform they already use.
film.creativeThesis: Show real Spectrum first, reveal the bankruptcy-specialized mechanism working beneath it, deliver three visible handled outcomes, then return to the same specialist and real Spectrum workflow.
film.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows. Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.
film.scriptSource: Final Demo Script Plan — original 45 words preserved, Jul 16, 2026
film.recommendation: Final Demo is the selected production candidate. It combines real Spectrum recognition, a human lender floor, the filing object, physical dashboard architecture, the internal BankruptcyWatch reveal, and three sequential handled outcomes. The eight earlier ideas remain supporting studies.
approvals: Third i finalizes the 22-second Final Demo plan and corrected internal segment allocation.
Produce the New Filing scene with the filing object as part of the same production pass.
Shaw approval for Spectrum interface use is secured; use exact cleaned Spectrum screenshots and masks for the bkWatch film.
One internal legacy screenshot containing visible customer/account information is rejected and must not enter the production package.
Jessica/Dylan and Shaw confirm live/showable Status, Tasks, Credit behavior, real-time wording, CTA, and factual claims.
Handled-state green, final Shaw + BankruptcyWatch logo lockup, combined interface frames, and all beginning and ending keyframe pairs receive approval.
Phase 7 locks delivery resolution, frame rate, color pipeline, audio and caption masters, and review/export formats.

ideas.title: Final Demo
ideas.concept: Real Spectrum becomes an intricate physical dashboard, reveals BankruptcyWatch working beneath it, resolves three handled outcomes, and returns to the same active lender workflow.
ideas.approval: Awaiting Third i plan/segment approval plus Spectrum screenshots, feature, color, logo, combined visuals, and keyframe approval checkpoints
ideas.strength: Best balance of real product proof, human credibility, native-integration clarity, and premium cinematic impact
ideas.distinctiveness: Real interface to physical system to internal mechanism to real interface

scenes.title: Scene 1 — Spectrum is already home
scenes.description: A simple over-the-shoulder push frames a woman working while approved real Spectrum fills more than 80 percent of the monitor and the lender floor remains active behind her.
scenes.direction: Use a stable filmed person and tracked exact interface; keep the real Shaw mark and safe sample data recognizable from the first frame. Production segment: F1.1.
scenes.transition: Cut from the dominant monitor to a desk-object-height three-quarter face angle.
scenes.purpose: Establish Spectrum as the familiar operating home base before any metaphor appears.
scenes.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation.

scenes.title: Scene 2 — The filing arrives
scenes.description: The physical filing object lowers perfectly vertically, hovers between the specialist and monitor, and begins moving toward Spectrum after one keyboard input.
scenes.direction: Allocate F2.1 to 2–4 seconds for descent, deceleration, and hover; allocate F2.2 to 4–5 seconds for the key input and departure. Produce the file-like New Filing label with the filing object during the same production pass.
scenes.transition: Follow the filing toward the prepared receiving region as the view returns to the monitor.
scenes.purpose: Give bankruptcy urgency a precise physical event without fear-based imagery.
scenes.script: Bankruptcy does not wait.

scenes.title: Scene 3 — Spectrum becomes physical
scenes.description: The approved real interface gains depth as the camera orbits and sweeps into a city-like physical dashboard. The filing clicks into a prepared slot, one blue pulse crosses the recessed valleys, and Status, Tasks, and Credit begin loading.
scenes.direction: Build F3.1 at 5–6 seconds, F3.2 at 6–8 seconds, and F3.3 at 8–10 seconds as controlled 3D animation from approved interface details and masks. Preserve safe data, panel geometry, live indicators, and one approved simplified slot region.
scenes.transition: Hold the side view as the complete Spectrum surface prepares to lift.
scenes.purpose: Make native automation feel physically engineered into the system the team already uses.
scenes.status: Draft instructions approved — need software screenshots before video
scenes.script: Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum,

scenes.title: Scene 4 — Why the platform knows
scenes.description: The Spectrum top layer rises as one rigid precision assembly, revealing an intricate black-and-blue BankruptcyWatch mechanism beneath it. One restrained pulse travels outward before the layer closes.
scenes.direction: Use F4.1 and F4.2 beginning and ending frames. Combine the approved BankruptcyWatch logo into the unmarked internal recess; avoid generic circuitry or science-fiction effects.
scenes.transition: The precision layer closes and aligns, setting up the completion hero.
scenes.purpose: Visually explain why Spectrum already knows without changing the original script order.
scenes.status: Draft instructions ready — need logos and animation frames before video
scenes.script: so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

scenes.title: Scene 5 — Handled-state hero
scenes.description: Status, Tasks, and Credit complete one at a time. Each check sends one green signal to a linked dashboard region; all three cards, paths, and targets remain illuminated as the camera widens.
scenes.direction: Build F5.1–F5.3 separately, preserve completed states, and use exact labels/checks in final editing after current-feature and handled-green approval.
scenes.transition: Continue the pullback as the physical dashboard resolves into the real working interface.
scenes.purpose: Turn the integration promise into three visible operational consequences.
scenes.status: Draft instructions ready — need feature and color approval before video
scenes.script: Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected.

scenes.title: Scene 6 — Same Spectrum, more capable
scenes.description: The view returns to the original over-the-shoulder workplace. The specialist performs another task while meaningful approved state changes occur in Spectrum and the active floor continues behind her.
scenes.direction: Use F6.1 as a stable filmed person, resolve to the approved alternate working interface, and add the transparent Shaw + BankruptcyWatch logo lockup only in final editing.
scenes.transition: End on the working environment rather than a detached generic end card.
scenes.purpose: Close the switching objection: the team already has Spectrum, and the workflow now has bankruptcy handled.
scenes.status: Instructions ready — need final software screenshots and logos before video
scenes.script: You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.

ideas.title: Already Inside Spectrum
ideas.concept: A direct product-proof control: one filing arrives, seats natively, and resolves into three handled Spectrum states.
ideas.approval: Retained as source study; not the current production candidate
ideas.recommendation: Standard benchmark for Final Demo proof clarity

scenes.title: Study 1, Scene 1 — Home base
scenes.description: A stable Spectrum view holds center while one filing approaches and a receiving point appears.
scenes.direction: Restrained product demonstration; Spectrum remains the visual anchor.
scenes.transition: Continuous native route into Scene 2.
scenes.purpose: Establish Spectrum as the trusted home base and introduce urgency without alarm.
scenes.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring

scenes.title: Study 1, Scene 2 — Native arrival
scenes.description: The filing travels through a direct native route and seats in the case area.
scenes.direction: Tier-one integration metaphor with exact interface treatment reserved for approved final editing.
scenes.transition: Direct movement into the handled state.
scenes.purpose: Show the filing signal entering through the native integration.
scenes.script: real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

scenes.title: Study 1, Scene 3 — Handled
scenes.description: Status, task, and reporting regions settle in order.
scenes.direction: Calm ordered proof; exact labels and logos remain added in final editing and feature-gated.
scenes.transition: End card treatment needs approval.
scenes.purpose: Show ordered workflow proof and land on the handled payoff.
scenes.script: Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.

ideas.title: The Moment It Lands
ideas.concept: A filing lands with controlled kinetic force, opening one blue seam that becomes three ordered outcomes.
ideas.approval: Retained as source study; not the current production candidate
ideas.recommendation: Kinetic source for the Final Demo filing click and pulse

scenes.title: Study 2, Scene 1 — Approach
scenes.description: A close-up account field sits on a Spectrum-derived plane as a filing descends toward one receiving slot.
scenes.direction: Precise close-up filming; no personal data or latency promise.
scenes.transition: Filing impact begins the seam reveal.
scenes.purpose: Establish home base and the arriving bankruptcy event.
scenes.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring

scenes.title: Study 2, Scene 2 — Impact
scenes.description: The filing lands edge-first; one blue seam opens and divides into three tracks.
scenes.direction: One decisive kinetic event; no representation of identity-match mechanics.
scenes.transition: Hard shape match from physical tracks to product regions.
scenes.purpose: Make the native automation event legible in one physical action.
scenes.script: real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

scenes.title: Study 2, Scene 3 — Consequence
scenes.description: Three physical tabs use a matching transition to three ordered Spectrum states.
scenes.direction: Product-region payoff with state labels added only in approved final editing.
scenes.transition: Hard shape match; end state holds.
scenes.purpose: Resolve the event into status, task, and reporting proof.
scenes.script: Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.

ideas.title: One Desk. No Detours.
ideas.concept: External court information folds into one native folio and docks without breaking the specialist's desk workflow.
ideas.approval: Retained as source study; not the current production candidate
ideas.recommendation: Human workflow source for the Final Demo opening and close

scenes.title: Study 3, Scene 1 — Outside pull
scenes.description: A specialist and Spectrum remain central while three translucent document planes enter the desk perimeter.
scenes.direction: One restrained camera arc around an anchored desk; court information stays abstract.
scenes.transition: Document planes prepare to hinge together.
scenes.purpose: Show the external complexity that historically pulled work away from Spectrum.
scenes.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring

scenes.title: Study 3, Scene 2 — Native fold
scenes.description: The planes hinge into one blue-bound folio and dock at the monitor edge.
scenes.direction: Physical integration metaphor; never imply zero human review.
scenes.transition: Folio docking motivates the single hand input.
scenes.purpose: Compress fragmented court information into a native workflow handoff.
scenes.script: real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

scenes.title: Study 3, Scene 3 — Same desk
scenes.description: One hand input settles task and status inside Spectrum; no extra app or operator appears.
scenes.direction: Continue the same restrained camera arc; approved interface detail is combined later.
scenes.transition: End on uninterrupted desk continuity.
scenes.purpose: Resolve the switching objection through a visible same-desk payoff.
scenes.script: Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.

ideas.title: Confidence Before Motion
ideas.concept: A precision iris accepts the filing, locks, then releases ordered action only after a deliberate beat.
ideas.approval: Retained as source study; not the current production candidate
ideas.recommendation: Precision and trust reference for Final Demo motion

scenes.title: Study 4, Scene 1 — Acceptance
scenes.description: Focus racks from Spectrum to a flush precision iris; the filing seats and the blue ring closes.
scenes.direction: Acceptance is a metaphor only; do not add unapproved verification wording.
scenes.transition: Hold the lock before releasing movement.
scenes.purpose: Frame the event as controlled rather than alarming.
scenes.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring

scenes.title: Study 4, Scene 2 — Controlled handoff
scenes.description: The locked signal exits on one narrow rail and seats in Spectrum.
scenes.direction: Minimal controlled movement; no accuracy or identifier claim.
scenes.transition: One-beat hold after the handoff.
scenes.purpose: Express native integration as a controlled route.
scenes.script: real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

scenes.title: Study 4, Scene 3 — Delayed action
scenes.description: After one held beat, status, task, and reporting move in order.
scenes.direction: Downstream motion is withheld until acceptance; exact features remain pending approval.
scenes.transition: Ordered settle into end state.
scenes.purpose: Show the three proof outcomes with a compliance-minded rhythm.
scenes.script: Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.

ideas.title: Built Beneath the Surface
ideas.concept: A tactile Spectrum surface separates by millimeters to reveal a purpose-built bkWatch mechanism underneath.
ideas.approval: Retained as the primary creative source for Final Demo
ideas.recommendation: Architectural source for the Final Demo internal reveal

scenes.title: Study 5, Scene 1 — Trusted surface
scenes.description: Raking light reveals a tactile Spectrum relief; its top separates by millimeters to expose a blue mechanism.
scenes.direction: Warm-white paper or resin above a black substrate; architecture is metaphor, not a literal technology stack.
scenes.transition: Camera follows the revealed channel beneath the surface.
scenes.purpose: Keep Spectrum visually trusted while revealing specialized capability underneath.
scenes.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring

scenes.title: Study 5, Scene 2 — Purpose-built route
scenes.description: The filing moves through a recessed channel below and raises one selected account tile flush.
scenes.direction: Use one precise blue mechanism; do not imply an exact identity-match method.
scenes.transition: Raised tile motivates an overhead move.
scenes.purpose: Make the native route feel engineered for bankruptcy work.
scenes.script: real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

scenes.title: Study 5, Scene 3 — Embossed proof
scenes.description: From overhead, status, task, and reporting embosses rise into calm order.
scenes.direction: Exact copy and logos are combined in final editing after feature approval.
scenes.transition: Calm overhead hold for the handled payoff.
scenes.purpose: Land three operational outcomes in a premium, ownable final frame.
scenes.script: Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.

ideas.title: The Workday Doesn't Break
ideas.concept: A continuous side-dolly preserves the specialist's daylight and hand rhythm while the bankruptcy event is handled in Spectrum.
ideas.approval: Retained as source study; not the current production candidate
ideas.recommendation: Continuity reference for the Final Demo return to work

scenes.title: Study 6, Scene 1 — Rhythm
scenes.description: A camera moving sideways follows the specialist, Spectrum, and one stripe of daylight as a filing reaches the monitor.
scenes.direction: No visible speech or personal data; performance remains measured.
scenes.transition: Camera and hand rhythm continue without a cut in energy.
scenes.purpose: Establish an attentive, uninterrupted working environment.
scenes.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring

scenes.title: Study 6, Scene 2 — Native handoff
scenes.description: The filing seats and one task advances while the specialist completes one measured hand movement.
scenes.direction: Do not imply zero review; automation supports the operator's continuity.
scenes.transition: Camera moving sideways continues into a wider frame.
scenes.purpose: Show native handling without breaking the operator's workflow.
scenes.script: real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

scenes.title: Study 6, Scene 3 — Continuity
scenes.description: The camera widens as status and reporting settle; daylight and hand rhythm continue with one operator.
scenes.direction: The emotional signature is protected attention, not celebration or a headcount claim.
scenes.transition: Continuous rhythm carries through the final frame.
scenes.purpose: Turn the handled payoff into visible workflow continuity.
scenes.script: Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.

ideas.title: The Update Writes Itself
ideas.concept: An editorial record corrects itself, opens onto Spectrum proof, and resolves into a concise evidence spread.
ideas.approval: Proof derivative only; quote placement remains gated
ideas.recommendation: Separate proof-led derivative after the main film

scenes.title: Study 7, Scene 1 — Correction
scenes.description: A text-free record sheet holds one misaligned gray field; a blue rule corrects its position.
scenes.direction: Use an abstract record only, never real borrower data.
scenes.transition: The corrected rule creates the cut-out shape reveal.
scenes.purpose: Translate bankruptcy risk into a controlled record correction.
scenes.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring

scenes.title: Study 7, Scene 2 — Product reveal
scenes.description: A cut-out shape paper window opens to a handled Spectrum state and a blank proof field.
scenes.direction: Any quote is short, approved, and added in final editing only.
scenes.transition: Window expands into an editorial spread.
scenes.purpose: Connect the correction metaphor to visible product proof.
scenes.script: real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

scenes.title: Study 7, Scene 3 — Proof spread
scenes.description: Product and proof field align into one editorial spread with three blue rules.
scenes.direction: Driveway logo or excerpt placement requires explicit approval.
scenes.transition: Hold as a proof-led end frame.
scenes.purpose: Package the three handled outcomes as sales evidence.
scenes.script: Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.

ideas.title: The Handled Orbit
ideas.concept: A filing token crosses a blue ingress arc, resolves into three states, and leaves a persistent handled marker as the next event arrives.
ideas.approval: Deployment derivative only; not the current production candidate
ideas.recommendation: Booth/site loop after the Final Demo is approved

scenes.title: Study 8, Scene 1 — Continuous arrival
scenes.description: A white filing token orbits a Spectrum-derived plane while one completed blue marker remains visible.
scenes.direction: Keep the metaphor silent-readable and abstract.
scenes.transition: The orbit reaches the blue ingress arc.
scenes.purpose: Establish recurring events around a stable Spectrum home base.
scenes.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring

scenes.title: Study 8, Scene 2 — Native crossing
scenes.description: The token crosses the blue ingress arc and enters the central plane.
scenes.direction: No exact latency promise; the crossing represents native integration.
scenes.transition: The crossing resolves into three settled states.
scenes.purpose: Make the native handoff legible within a loopable system.
scenes.script: real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

scenes.title: Study 8, Scene 3 — Persistent handling
scenes.description: Three states settle; the completed marker remains while the next token reaches its opening position.
scenes.direction: The loop continues without resetting the handled state.
scenes.transition: Seamless loop to Scene 1 opening position.
scenes.purpose: Show persistent handling and prepare a continuous booth or site loop.
scenes.script: Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.

projects.source: shaw-bkWatch Working Memory, Final Demo Plan, D2 Comparative Storyboards, and instruction inventory — updated Jul 16, 2026
projects.creativeVision: Show real Spectrum first, reveal the bankruptcy-specialized mechanism working beneath it, deliver three visible handled outcomes, then return to the same specialist and real Spectrum workflow.

keyMessaging.title: Native integration
keyMessaging.detail: Teams can add specialized bankruptcy automation without leaving the loan management platform they already use.

keyMessaging.title: Real product proof
keyMessaging.detail: Show actual Spectrum UI, real bankruptcy workflow, and visible handled outcomes — not abstract claims.

keyMessaging.title: Controlled cinematic impact
keyMessaging.detail: Premium visual quality that elevates the product without distracting from its real functionality.
